summary: PR #2542 added a dedicated MetroTRK exception vector table split at src/MetroTRK/__exception.s, represented the layout-sensitive .init region in assembly, registered the split range in config/GALE01/splits.txt, and updated the Nix configure invocation to provide binutils from devkitppc so dtk would not download assembler tooling in the sandboxed Nix build.

key_files.role: New assembly source for the MetroTRK exception vector table in .init, using local macros, labels, fixed gaps, embedded string data, and exception redirect instructions.
evidence_refs: pr_body: dedicated split for MetroTRK exception vector table as src/MetroTRK/__exception.s
diff: new file src/MetroTRK/__exception.s with .section .init, trk_redirect/trk_tlb_redirect macros, gTRKInterruptVectorTable, exception labels, .skip gaps, and gTRKInterruptVectorTableEnd

key_files.role: Registers the new MetroTRK/__exception.s split in the .init section from 0x80003298 to 0x800051CC before MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.c.
evidence_refs: diff: added MetroTRK/__exception.s: comment:0 with .init start:0x80003298 end:0x800051CC

key_files.role: Passes --binutils ${devkitppc}/bin to configure.py so the Nix build can use packaged binutils instead of a network download.
evidence_refs: issuecomment-4548211900: r-burns explains Nix failure was due to new binutils dependency and sandboxed non-networked build
issuecomment-4555655501: r-burns says dtk looks like it just needs the assembler and packaged binutils is available for Nix

what_changed.text: Added a dedicated MetroTRK exception vector table assembly file at src/MetroTRK/__exception.s.

The new assembly file places the vector table in .init and defines gTRKInterruptVectorTable and gTRKInterruptVectorTableEnd around a contiguous block of embedded string data, exception branch/redirect code, labels, and .skip-sized gaps.
evidence_refs: diff: .section .init, .global gTRKInterruptVectorTable, .asciz "Metrowerks Target Resident Kernel for PowerPC", .skip entries, exception comments, .global gTRKInterruptVectorTableEnd

what_changed.text: Introduced small assembly macros trk_redirect and trk_tlb_redirect for repeated exception redirect sequences.
evidence_refs: pr_body: cleaned up the source with small assembly macros
diff: .macro trk_redirect vector and .macro trk_tlb_redirect vector

what_changed.text: Added a GALE01 split entry for MetroTRK/__exception.s covering .init 0x80003298 through 0x800051CC.
evidence_refs: diff: config/GALE01/splits.txt added MetroTRK/__exception.s .init start:0x80003298 end:0x800051CC

what_changed.text: Updated the Nix configure command to pass --binutils ${devkitppc}/bin.
The decomp report recorded one new matched unit, main/MetroTRK/__exception pad_00_80003298_init, adding 7988 matched/linked bytes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report for GALE01 lists matched code +7988 bytes and one new match for main/MetroTRK/__exception

smart_moves.text: Used assembly rather than C for a layout-sensitive exception vector region that mixes bytes, fixed-size gaps, labels, and instructions.
evidence_refs: pr_body: represented as assembly because region is layout-sensitive and not ordinary gameplay code
diff: __exception.s uses .asciz, .balign, .skip, labels, branch instructions, and macros in one .init block

smart_moves.text: Kept the split boundary explicit in splits.txt adjacent to MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.c, preserving the .init range ownership.
evidence_refs: diff: splits.txt inserts MetroTRK/__exception.s .init start/end before MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.c

smart_moves.text: Resolved the Nix build issue by wiring an existing packaged binutils path into configure.py instead of relying on dtk to download binaries during a sandboxed build.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For exception/vector-table regions that are inherently layout-sensitive and combine executable instructions with embedded bytes, exact gaps, and labels, assembly may be the reviewable representation rather than forcing C.
evidence_refs: pr_body: region is layout-sensitive, not ordinary gameplay code, mixes embedded bytes, fixed-size gaps, labels, and instructions
diff: __exception.s has .section .init, .asciz, .skip gaps, labels, branches, rfi, and SPR instructions

decomp_lessons.lesson: Adding assembly sources can introduce assembler/binutils requirements that ordinary local builds may fetch automatically but reproducible Nix builds must receive explicitly.
evidence_refs: issuecomment-4548211900: Nix refused dtk's on-the-fly binary download because the build is sandboxed and non-networked

decomp_lessons.lesson: A focused split for a contiguous low-level region can produce a measurable matched-code gain when the split range and source representation align with the binary layout.
evidence_refs: diff: split range 0x80003298-0x800051CC added for MetroTRK/__exception.s
text_corpus: bot report lists one new match and +7988 matched bytes

naming_conventions.name: gTRKInterruptVectorTable
naming_conventions.lesson: The vector table start label was named as a TRK global table symbol.

naming_conventions.name: gTRKInterruptVectorTableEnd
naming_conventions.lesson: The end of the table was given a paired End label.

naming_conventions.name: trk_redirect / trk_tlb_redirect
naming_conventions.lesson: Repeated redirect sequences were factored into lowercase assembly macros with TRK-specific names.

naming_conventions.name: .L_restore_cr_\@ / .L_perf_monitor
naming_conventions.lesson: Local labels describe narrow control-flow roles in the assembly block; macro-local labels use \@ for uniqueness.
evidence_refs: diff: .L_perf_monitor in performance monitor / AltiVec area

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a dedicated assembly file for the MetroTRK exception vector table instead of C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.why: The PR body states the region is layout-sensitive and mixes embedded bytes, fixed-size gaps, labels, and instructions.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use .skip directives to preserve exact vector spacing between exception handlers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.why: The assembly block contains fixed skip sizes after each exception vector sequence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use macros for repeated exception redirection sequences while keeping special cases explicit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.why: Most exception entries invoke trk_redirect or trk_tlb_redirect, while reset, machine check, and performance-monitor/AltiVec areas include explicit branches or extra instructions.
evidence_refs: diff: Machine Check includes icbi/dcbi sequence before trk_redirect 0x200
diff: Performance Monitor / AltiVec section uses b .L_perf_monitor and separate redirects

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add split metadata before the following MetroTRK .init code so the new assembly owns the exact vector-table range.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.why: splits.txt inserts MetroTRK/__exception.s .init 0x80003298-0x800051CC immediately before MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.c starting at 0x800051CC.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For Nix, pass an explicit binutils path to configure.py when assembly support requires an assembler.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.why: Review discussion identified sandboxed Nix failure from dtk attempting to download binutils, and the final diff passes --binutils ${devkitppc}/bin.

review_feedback.feedback: Asked why Nix was consistently failing on this PR but not others.

Explained that the PR added a binutils dependency; regular builds could download dtk binaries on the fly, but Nix refused network downloads because it is sandboxed and non-networked for reproducibility.
Questioned whether requiring all of binutils was worthwhile for an unused MetroTRK component, but offered to update Nix if the PR proceeded.
Asked whether dtk needed all of binutils or only the assembler, and suggested making a solution work.
Concluded dtk appeared to need only the assembler, preferred packaged upstream binutils for Nix over precompiled binaries, and said the Nix fix was simple.
Approved after the CI/Nix issue was resolved, stating the CI should be all green.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: review by r-burns, state APPROVED, body says CI should be all green
metadata_json: PR number, title, author, merged state, timestamps, changed files
pr_body: rationale for assembly representation and dedicated split
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report, issue comments, and approval review
notes: Evidence directly supports changed files, split range, assembly structure, Nix binutils fix, and matched-code report.
The slice has no inline review comments; review feedback is from issue comments and one approval review.
The PR body references OOT GC Decomp as prior art, but this intake does not infer equivalence beyond the stated reference.
The bot report claims a new match and +7988 bytes; no independent build or objdiff output beyond that report is present in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use assembly for layout-sensitive exception vector tables
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2542 supports a scoped lesson that MetroTRK-style exception vector regions in .init can be better represented as assembly when they mix embedded bytes, fixed-size gaps, labels, and instructions in one contiguous layout-sensitive block.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Assembly additions may require explicit Nix binutils wiring
accepted_candidate_records.text: When a PR introduces assembly that causes dtk to need assembler tooling, sandboxed Nix builds may fail unless configure.py receives an explicit packaged binutils path.

source_update_candidates.title: MetroTRK exception vector split path and range
source_update_candidates.text: config/GALE01/splits.txt maps MetroTRK/__exception.s to .init start:0x80003298 end:0x800051CC; source path is src/MetroTRK/__exception.s.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible durable path/split ownership fact for later source-scope lookup.

source_update_candidates.title: Nix configure passes binutils from devkitppc
source_update_candidates.text: .nix/melee.nix passes --binutils ${devkitppc}/bin to configure.py for the Nix build.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential build-configuration fact explaining why binutils is supplied explicitly in the Nix environment.

source_update_candidates.title: Assembly is acceptable for layout-sensitive low-level vector regions
source_update_candidates.text: Consider a narrowly scoped standard noting that low-level regions such as exception vector tables may be represented in assembly when PR evidence shows the region is layout-sensitive and combines embedded bytes, fixed-size gaps, labels, and instructions. This should not become a blanket permission for inline assembly in ordinary decomp source.
source_update_candidates.reason: Current standards emphasize avoiding inline assembly as an exception; this PR may justify a source-owned clarification for dedicated assembly files in non-ordinary, layout-sensitive low-level regions.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize this PR into approval for arbitrary assembly in gameplay code; the stated rationale is specific to a layout-sensitive MetroTRK .init exception vector table.
Do not claim reviewers accepted a broad binutils dependency policy; discussion only supports that this PR's Nix fix used packaged binutils/devkitppc and that dtk appeared to need the assembler.
Do not treat the OOT GC Decomp reference as proof of original Melee source style; it is only cited as a reference in the PR body.
Do not independently claim full CI success beyond the approval comment saying CI should be all green and the PR merged.
